page: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v3 is an ESv3-family virtual machine configured with 4 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, the instance features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The CPU architecture is x86_64, incorporating 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache. It supports hardware-assisted virtualization and instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 131 GB, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are included.

Benchmark results indicate that the compute performance of this instance is generally constrained. In stress-ng and Geekbench tests, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% tier. Similarly, memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks, along with single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, register in the weak category. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is rated as poor, falling into the bottom 10%. However, the Passmark memory score and LLM inference text generation speed for a small 135M model achieve average ratings within the middle 50% tier. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput remain in the bottom 25% tier.

The E8-4s_v3 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require a large memory footprint but do not demand high CPU throughput. The primary tradeoff is the weak compute performance relative to the substantial 64.0 GB memory allocation, as evidenced by the low CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark scores. This resource density makes the instance suitable for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads such as caching layers, small database operations, or lightweight text and image processing tasks that fit entirely within memory.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v3 is an ESv3-family virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz with 2 physical cores and hyper-threading, this x86_64 instance includes 131 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. It does not include a GPU. Performance benchmarks indicate constrained compute capabilities, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and memory bandwidth tests generally scoring in the bottom 25%. However, the Passmark memory score and small-model LLM text generation speed reach average tiers. The high memory density relative to CPU power makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-compute tasks. It is suitable for caching layers, small database operations, and memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive CPU processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v3 is an ESv3 virtual machine with 4 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 131 GB local storage. It offers high memory density but exhibits constrained CPU and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v3 features 4 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 131 GB storage, offering high memory density with constrained compute performance.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for memory-bound workloads with moderate compute requirements.

Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz with x86_64 architecture
Bundled with 131 GB of local storage and Premium Storage capability
Average performance in Passmark memory and small-model LLM text generation
